uk: Неділя 12-та по Зісланні Святого Духа. Глас 3. Єв. 1.
🕃 Перенесення з Едеси до Царгороду нерукотворного образа Господа Бога і Спаса нашого Ісуса Христа, названого святим обрусом
saints.en: 11th Sunday after Pentecost. Translation from Edessa to Constantinople of the Not-Made-by-Hands Image of our Lord, God and Savior Jesus Christ

rubrics:
Вечірня: «Блажен муж». На «Господи, взиваю» 10 стихири: 3 воскресні, 3 свята і 4 Образу; Сл: Образу, І нині: богородичний-догмат 3-го гласу. Вхід. Прокімен дня. Стиховня воскресна, Сл: Образу, І нині: свята. По «Отче наш»: тропар воскресний, Сл.: Образу, І нині: свята. Відпуст великий недільний з помин. свята.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь» тропар воскресний (2), Сл.: Образу, І нині: свята. Катизми черг., сідал. неділ., «Ангельський собор», іпакой, степенна та прокімен 3-го гласу. Єв. – Мт. 116 зач.; 28, 16-20. «Воскресіння Христове». Пс. 50. Стихира недільна. Канон воскресний, свята і Образу. Катавасія - «Прикрашена». По 3-ій – конд.-ікос свята і сідал. Образу, Сл., І нині: свята. По 6-ій – конд.-ікос Образу. На 9-ій – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – «Свят Господь Бог наш», світил. воскресний, Сл.: Образу, І нині: свята. Хвалитних стихир 8: 4 воскресних і 4 Образу з двома їхніми приспівами, Сл.: стихира євангельська, І нині: «Преблагословенна». По вел. славослов'ї тропар «Сьогодні спасіння світу». Відпуст великий недільний з помин. свята.
Часи: на всіх тропар воскресний Слава: на 1-му і 6-му – ще й Образу, 3-му і 9-му – свята. Кондаки: на 1-му – неділ., на 3-му – Образу, на 6-му – свята, і 9-му – воскресний.
Літургія: антифони: два перших – воскресні, а третій – звичайний з воскресним приспівом, а також і «Прийдіте, поклонімся». Тропар воскресний, Образу і свята, конд. воскресний, Сл.: Образу, І нині: свята. Апостол, Євангеліє, прокімен і алилуя. Ряд.: Ап. – 1 Кор. 158 зач.; 15, 1-11. Єв. – Мт. 79 зач.; 19, 16-26. Образу: Ап. – Кол. 250 зач.; 1, 12-18. Єв. – Лк. 48 зач. (від половини); 9, 51-56; 10, 22-24. Причасний «Хваліте» і свята.

troparion_en.text: We venerate Your most pure image, O Good One, / and ask forgiveness of our transgressions, O Christ God. / Of Your own will You were pleased to ascend the Cross in the flesh / to deliver Your creatures from bondage to the Enemy. / Therefore with thanksgiving we cry aloud to You: / “You have filled all with joy, O our Savior, / by coming to save the world.”

kontakion_en.text: Uncircumscribed Word of the Father / as we behold the victorious image of Your true incarnation, / not made by hands, but divinely wrought / in Your ineffable and divine dispensation towards us, / we honor it with veneration!

matched_oca_saint: Translation of the Image “Not-Made-By-Hands” of our Lord Jesus Christ from Edessa to Constantinople, the Third “Feast of the Savior in August”

oca_saints.name: Afterfeast of the Dormition of the Mother of God

troparion.text: In giving birth you preserved your virginity, / in falling asleep you did not forsake the world, O Theotokos. / You were translated to life, O Mother of Life, / and by your prayers, you deliver our souls from death.

kontakion.text: Neither the tomb, nor death could hold the Theotokos, / who is constant in prayer and our firm hope in her intercessions. / For being the Mother of Life, / she was translated to life by the One who dwelt in her virginal womb.

oca_saints.name: Translation of the Image “Not-Made-By-Hands” of our Lord Jesus Christ from Edessa to Constantinople, the Third “Feast of the Savior in August”

troparion.text: We venerate Your most pure image, O Good One, / and ask forgiveness of our transgressions, O Christ God. / Of Your own will You were pleased to ascend the Cross in the flesh / to deliver Your creatures from bondage to the Enemy. / Therefore with thanksgiving we cry aloud to You: / “You have filled all with joy, O our Savior, / by coming to save the world.”

kontakion.text: Uncircumscribed Word of the Father / as we behold the victorious image of Your true incarnation, / not made by hands, but divinely wrought / in Your ineffable and divine dispensation towards us, / we honor it with veneration!

troparion.text: The instruments of torture have made a way for you to Heaven, O soldier of Christ Diomḗdēs, /  you defeated the devil’s intrigues and became distinguished in Heaven with Christ,  / pray for those who honor your memory with faith.

kontakion.text: As a true and invincible Martyr,  /  you became the guardian of the heavenly mysteries of piety and grace, O wondrous Diomḗdēs,   / having overcome the torturer and becoming victorious,   / you were honored with crowns of victory.

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Cherimon, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Icon of the Mother of God of Saint Theodore

troparion.text: Today, the renowned city of Kostroma and the entire Russian land rejoice joyfully,  / calling all God-loving Christian people to the glorious triumph of the Mother of God /  because of the arrival of her wonderworking and much-healing Icon: /  for today a great and most radiant sun has appeared to us.  / Come all of God's chosen people, the new Israel, to the fountain of healing, /  for it pours forth to us the inexhaustible mercy of the Most Holy Theotokos  / and delivers all Christian cities and lands from the malice of their enemies.  / But, All-Merciful Lady, Virgin Theotokos, Sovereign Lady,  / by your great mercy, save our country, the bishops, and your people /  from all their misfortunes that we may cry out to you,   / “Rejoice, O Virgin, the boast of Christians.”

kontakion.text: We your servants offer prayers of thanksgiving to you, O Theotokos, /  for all the good things you have done for our city;  / we cry out to you from the depths of our souls, and bowing down we pray: /  “O Sovereign Lady, do not cease to intercede with your Son and our God  / to grant every good thing and salvation to all who cry out to you with faith and love,  / ‘Rejoice, O Virgin, the boast of Christians.’”

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Christopher, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Christopher;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Isaac, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Isaac;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Joseph, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Joseph;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Joachim, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Νikόdēmos, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

Your holy martyr Stamatius, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Stamatius;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Gerasimus the New Ascetic of Cephalonia

troparion.text:
Let us the faithful praise the divine Gerasimos, / who is the protector of the Orthodox, and an angel in the body,
and a newly appeared God-bearing wonderworker; / for he has worthily received from God the unfailing grace of
healing; / he restores the sick, and heals those possessed by demons; / therefore, he pours forth healings upon those who honor him.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Today the Virgin...”)
Today the island of Cephalonia /  with sacred songs of thanksgiving, / calls upon all the Orthodox to extol the newly appeared boast and glory of Orthodoxy, /  the divine Gerasimos, / who is truly her deliverer and champion.

oca_saints.name: Saint Constantine Brancoveanu and those with him

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Constantine and his companions, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls
